title: Quiz: Quantum Classification
description: Test your understanding of quantum classification methods

questions.question: In a quantum binary classifier, how is the class label typically determined?

options.text: By measuring the phase of the quantum state
By measuring one or more qubits and interpreting the outcome
By counting the number of gates applied

questions.explanation: Quantum classifiers determine class labels by measuring qubits - typically the probability of measuring |0⟩ vs |1⟩ determines the predicted class.

questions.question: What is angle encoding in quantum classification?

options.text: Encoding data as rotation angles in quantum gates

questions.explanation: Angle encoding maps classical data values to rotation angles in quantum gates, e.g., RY(θ·x) where x is the data value.

questions.question: For multi-class classification with k classes, how many qubits are needed for class encoding?
questions.explanation: To encode k classes, you need ⌈log₂(k)⌉ qubits since n qubits can represent 2^n different measurement outcomes.

questions.question: Quantum kernel methods require training variational parameters.
questions.explanation: Quantum kernel methods use a fixed quantum feature map to compute kernel values. Training happens in the classical kernel machine (e.g., SVM), not in the quantum circuit.

questions.question: What loss function is commonly used for binary quantum classification?
questions.explanation: Binary cross-entropy is the standard loss for binary classification, measuring the difference between predicted probabilities and true labels.

questions.question: What is the quantum kernel between two data points x₁ and x₂?

options.text: The dot product of x₁ and x₂
The fidelity between feature map states |ψ(x₁)⟩ and |ψ(x₂)⟩
The Euclidean distance between x₁ and x₂
The number of gates needed to transform x₁ to x₂

questions.explanation: The quantum kernel K(x₁, x₂) = |⟨ψ(x₁)|ψ(x₂)⟩|² measures the squared overlap (fidelity) between quantum feature map states.

questions.question: What is an advantage of quantum kernel classifiers over variational classifiers?

options.text: They don't suffer from barren plateaus during training
They don't require any classical processing

questions.explanation: Quantum kernel methods use fixed feature maps, so there's no variational optimization that could suffer from barren plateaus. Training happens classically.

questions.question: Which are valid approaches for quantum classification? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: Variational classifiers (a), kernel methods (b), and quantum SVMs (d) are established quantum classification approaches. Quantum decision trees (c) are less common and more experimental.
